data.title: Laureate Education Non-GAAP EPS of $0.76 misses by $0.03, revenue of $541.4M beats by $14.3M
data.description:
• Laureate Education press release (LAUR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.76 misses by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $541.4M (+27.9% Y/Y) beats by $14.3M.
  • New enrollments for full-year 2025 increased 8% compared to new enrollment activity for full-year 2024, and total enrollments were up 5%. New and total enrollments in Peru increased 13% and 7%, respectively, compared to 2024. New and total enrollments in Mexico increased 5% and 4%, respectively, compared to 2024.

data.title: DT Midstream Non-GAAP EPS of $1.08 misses by $0.07; initiates FY26 and FY27 outlook
data.description:
• DT Midstream press release (DTM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.08 misses by $0.07.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $293 million.
  • “Our Adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2026 is $1.155 to $1.225 billion, representing 6% annual growth from our 2025 original guidance. Our 2027 Adjusted EBITDA early outlook range is $1.225 to $1.295 billion.”

  • David Slater, Executive Chairman and CEO noted the following significant business updates:

    • Increased organic project backlog by approximately 50 percent to $3.4 billion over the next 5 years, with pipeline projects comprising 75% of the backlog
    • Increased dividend by 7% from fourth quarter 2025 to $0.88 per share, to be paid on April 15, 2026 to stockholders of record on March 16, 2026
    • Reached final investment decisions on an expansion of Viking Gas Transmission and the next phase of the interstate pipeline modernization program

data.title: Navios Maritime Non-GAAP EPS of $3.40 beats by $1.27, revenue of $365.5M beats by $49.5M
data.description:
• Navios Maritime press release (NMM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.40 beats by $1.27.
  • Revenue of $365.5M (+9.9% Y/Y) beats by $49.5M.

data.title: Regeneron granted FDA priority review for rare bone disorder therapy
data.description:
• Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) announced on Thursday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) has granted priority review for its marketing application for garetosmab, a treatment targeted at an ultra-rare bone growth disorder.
  • The regulator has assigned a target action date in August 2026 in connection with REGN's Biologics License Application, which seeks the U.S. approval of garetosmab for adults with fibrodysplasia ossificans progressiva ((FOP)).
  • FOP, characterized by abnormal bone formation in connective tissues such as muscles, leads to loss of mobility as patients experience skeletal deformity and dysfunction in these structures.
  • An estimated 900 people globally are diagnosed with FOP, with many of them becoming wheelchair-bound by the age of 30.
  • The BLA is backed by data from Regeneron’s (REGN) Phase 3 OPTIMA trial, which indicated that garetosmab can reduce FOP lesions by up to 94% over 56 weeks.

data.title: Brady Non-GAAP EPS of $1.09 in-line, revenue of $384.1M beats by $5.51M; raises FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Brady press release (BRC): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.09 in-line.
  • Revenue of $384.1M (+7.7% Y/Y) beats by $5.51M.
  • Organic sales increased 1.6 percent, acquisitions increased sales 2.3 percent and foreign currency translation increased sales 3.8 percent.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities increased to $53.3 million in the second quarter of fiscal 2026 compared to $39.6 million in the second quarter of last year.
  • Fiscal 2026 Guidance:The Company raised the low end of its GAAP earnings per diluted Class A Nonvoting Common Share guidance for the year ending July 31, 2026 from $4.57 to $4.82 per share, to $4.62 to $4.82 per share. The Company raised the low end of its Adjusted Diluted EPS* guidance for the year ending July 31, 2026 from $4.90 to $5.15 per share to $4.95 to $5.15 per share vs. consensus of $5.05.

data.title: Freeport signs agreement with Indonesia to extend Grasberg mining rights through 2041
data.description:
Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) said late Wednesday that it has entered into a memorandum of understanding with the Indonesia government to extend its Grasberg permit for the life of the resource, ensuring long-term access to one of the world's largest copper and gold mines.

Special mining rights for Freeport's (FCX) Indonesian unit, currently valid until 2031, will be amended to allow operations through 2041, at which time the company's holding in Freeport Indonesia would fall to 37% following the transfer of a 12% stake to the government, according to the preliminary agreement.

Freeport Indonesia (FCX) will continue to prioritize domestic downstreaming through local sales of refined copper and other products and will be positioned to broaden its marketing of refined copper to the U.S. if the country requires additional supplies of copper, the agreement also says.

Grasberg, located in Indonesia's Central Papua province, is the world's second-largest copper mine, accounting for ~3% of global production.

data.title: Discovery Silver Non-GAAP EPS of $0.14, revenue of $274.24M; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Discovery Silver press release (DSVSF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.14.
  • Revenue of $274.24M (-58.0% Y/Y).
  • 6% GROWTH IN GOLD PRODUCTION
    • 66,718 oz produced versus 63,154 oz in Q3 2025
  • 12% IMPROVEMENT IN OPERATING CASH COSTS
    • Operating cash costs1 of $1,185/oz sold compared to $1,339/oz in Q3 2025
  • AISC REFLECTS HIGHER SUSTAINING CAPITAL1
    • AISC/oz1,2 averaged $2,034; Site-level AISC/oz3 averaged $1,824
  • INVESTING TO IMPROVE AND GROW PORCUPINE
    • Sustaining capital expenditures1 of $33.8M, with growth capital expenditures1 of $66.1M versus $20.8M and $44.4M, respectively, in Q3 2025
  • STRONG CASH FLOW FROM GOLD SALES
    • Net cash from operating activities of $163.2M; Free cash flow1 of $67.9M
  • 20% GROWTH IN CASH POSITION
    • Cash at December 31, 2025, of $410.7M, with $250M of liquidity from an undrawn revolving credit facility and $100M accordion feature.

data.title: UL Solutions Non-GAAP EPS of $0.53 beats by $0.08, revenue of $789M beats by $6.64M
data.description:
• UL Solutions press release (ULS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.53 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $789M (+6.8% Y/Y) beats by $6.64M.
  • Strong revenue growth of 6.4% to $3,053 million, including 6.2% organic growth
  • Net income of $345 million, including a $35 million pre-tax restructuring charge, unchanged from prior period, Adjusted Net Income of $423 million increased 17.2%
  • Diluted earnings per share of $1.60 decreased 1.2%, Adjusted Diluted Earnings Per Share of $1.99 increased 17.1%
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $792 million increased 20.7%, Adjusted EBITDA margin of 25.9% expanded 300 basis points
  • Record net cash flows provided by operating activities of $600 million

data.title: Cushman & Wakefield beats Q4 topline estimates
data.description:
• Cushman & Wakefield press release (CWK): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 in-line.
  • Revenue of $2.9B (+11.5% Y/Y) beats by $70M.
  • Net loss was $22.4 million for the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to net income of $112.9 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: N-able Non-GAAP EPS of $0.06 misses by $0.04, revenue of $130.3M beats by $3.23M
data.description:
• N-able press release (NABL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.06 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $130.3M (+11.8% Y/Y) beats by $3.23M.
  • Subscription revenue of $129.0 million, representing 12.1% year-over-year growth, or 9.3% year-over-year growth on a constant currency basis.
  • Total ARR of $539.7 million, representing 11.9% year-over-year growth, or 7.7% year-over-year growth on a constant currency basis.
  • GAAP gross margin of 76.2% and non-GAAP gross margin of 79.8%.
